_comment: Shared accept/reject fixture for a yt-dlp format id: a concrete format id, optionally `+`-combined for a merged video+audio selection. Asserted from both sides so the two independent validators cannot drift apart: TypeScript isValidYtDlpFormatId (src/services/yt-dlp-format-rules.ts, src/services/yt-dlp-format-rules.test.ts) and Rust is_valid_format_id (src-tauri/src/services/yt_dlp_download/mod.rs). The frontend's buildMergedFormats synthesizes the `<video>+<audio>` merged selector, and the backend re-validates every format id it receives against this exact rule (is_valid_format_id) before resolving it against the fetched metadata (resolve_format_has_video). This fixture pins that the shape the frontend builds is the shape the backend accepts, so a change to one side's rule that the other did not follow fails a test rather than surfacing as a raw backend error after the round trip. Add a case to both sides at once by editing this one file.
